sections.title: Materials
sections.instructions: Listen to the word, then type it correctly.

words.hint: Erasers are made of this.
Bottles are often made of this.
A hard, shiny material used for tools.
Windows are made of this transparent material.
Warm fabric that comes from sheep.
Something that bends easily without breaking.
The opposite of flexible; stiff and hard.
Special clothes worn for a performance or party.
A maze with many confusing paths.
Light, soft covering on birds.
The opposite of light in weight.
A surface with no bumps; the opposite of rough.
A surface that is not smooth; uneven.
A very sad story or event.
A funny play or film that makes you laugh.
Craft material made from paper and glue.

sections.title: Directions & Technology
sections.instructions: Listen to the word, then type it correctly.

words.hint: Go in a line without turning.
On the other side; facing something.
In the middle of two things.
Going in one side and out the other.
A circular junction where traffic goes around.
Software you use to visit websites.
To save a file from the internet to your device.
A secret word you type to log in.
A cable that gives power to your phone.
The part of a computer you type on.
A mountain that can erupt with lava.
A very dry area with lots of sand.
The study of the Earth and its features.
Related to governments and how countries are run.
Related to the body, or to real objects you can touch.

sections.title: Jobs & Adjectives
sections.instructions: Listen to the word, then type it correctly.

words.hint: A person who takes photos for a living.
A person who writes news stories.
A performer who does gymnastics and stunts.
A person who creates the look of things.
A person who serves food in a restaurant.
Something that keeps your attention; not boring.
Not kind or welcoming to others.
Liked by many people.
Not afraid; showing courage.
Quick to learn and understand; smart.
Depending on something else; an 'if' situation.
A scientist who studies ancient objects and ruins.
A building with blades that turn in the wind.
A show or act in front of an audience.
The way someone or something looks.
